_resumeUrl_note: Canonical live/production URL of this résumé. Shown as a globe link in the top bar (opens in a new tab), as a 'Resume' item in the mobile contacts menu, in the print contacts, and in the Markdown (.md) export. Leave "" to hide it everywhere. Overridden at build time by the VITE_RESUME_URL env var when set (e.g. http://resume.local for a dev/staging build).
_certPlaceholder_note: Fallback image shown in place of a certificate screenshot that can't load (the offline single-file build streams cert images from <root>/assets/certs/, so offline they fail). It stays inlined in the offline single-file build; clicking it opens the prod-URL certificate in a new tab.

seo._note: noindex: true asks search engines AND AI crawlers not to index this page (injects robots + noai meta tags at load). Set to false to allow indexing.

contacts.introMessage: Hello! I'm interested in your skills — let's work together. Could we find a time to talk?

cta.line: Can you imagine what I could build for you after five years working together?
cta.sub: Write to me — I'd love to hear your thoughts.

testimonial.quote: I worked closely with Egor on key product features. His technical depth is exceptional — every architectural discussion with him is a chance to learn. Recently he pioneered AI-driven development workflows, benchmarking models and agents to streamline our engineering processes, which directly improved code quality and team efficiency. Egor plans every architectural detail before writing a line of code. I highly recommend him to any forward-thinking engineering team.
testimonial.name: Anton Kostylov

_skillAliases_note: Folds different spellings of the SAME tech onto one token so clicking a skill highlights every project that used it. Trailing versions are already stripped automatically (Vue.js 2.6.14 -> vue.js, Laravel 9 -> laravel); only add entries here when the BASE word differs across skills vs projects. Keys/values are lowercased, version-stripped bases.
_trendSkills_note: Recruiter-facing CURRENT-trend / must-have skills → weight (1-5; 5 = headline must-have today, 1 = nice-to-have). Drives the quiet accent on skill chips AND decides which 'NEW' items get the bold pill (top 3 by weight per group/project/cert) vs a small dot. Applied everywhere: the skills grid, timeline project tech, and certificate/conference skills. Names are matched to chips alias-aware & version-agnostic ('Vue' matches 'Vue.js'), but prefer the exact skill display name. HOW TO UPDATE when you add a new project: skim its technologies and bump/add the in-demand ones for your target roles (keep ~5 strong entries per role group so the highlight stays selective). Leave {} to disable (the grid falls back to years-of-experience ranking; no accent elsewhere). Global — no per-era/per-role weighting yet.
_trendReorder_note: true = trend-weighted skills also physically move to the TOP of their group in the skills grid (sub-items stay under their parent). false (default) = highlight/accent only, original order preserved.

intro.caption: Hi, I'm Egor — here's a little about how I work.
